params.name: Irises
params.description:
On May 25, 1889 AD, Van Gogh painted this canvas in the last year of his life.
Irises, a canvas by the famous painter Van Gogh, is known as one of his greatest works during the Saint-Remy period. Van Gogh painted this still life in the psychiatric hospital in Saint-Remy. For him, the painting was mainly a study in color. He set out to achieve a powerful color contrast. By placing the purple flowers against a yellow background, he made the decorative forms stand out even more strongly. As one …
